project.title: Kettle & Kin
project.summary: A booking-driven website for a boutique café chain with live table reservations and a slick, on-brand experience.
project.overview: A growing café chain wanted one beautiful website that captured their cozy brand and let guests book a table in seconds across all their locations.
project.challenge: Reservations were handled over the phone and DMs, which meant missed bookings and a frustrating experience. Their old site was slow, dated, and didn't reflect the in-store vibe.
project.solution: We designed a warm, photography-led site in React and Tailwind, then wired up a live bookings API so guests can reserve a table by location in real time. Menus and events update through a simple content layer the team manages themselves.

testimonial.quote: Bookings now run on autopilot and the site actually feels like walking into one of our cafés. Our staff got their phones back.
testimonial.author: Operations Lead, Kettle & Kin

next.title: Verde Living
next.summary: A sustainable home-goods marketplace with multi-vendor support, smart search, and a checkout that just works.
next.overview: Verde set out to build the go-to marketplace for sustainable home products in India — connecting eco-conscious shoppers with dozens of independent makers in one trusted place.
next.challenge: Launching a multi-vendor marketplace means juggling vendor onboarding, fast search across thousands of products, and a checkout that handles split orders — all without overwhelming shoppers.
next.solution: We built a Next.js commerce platform with a self-serve vendor portal, Algolia-powered instant search and filtering, and a Razorpay checkout that splits payouts automatically. The catalog scales effortlessly as new makers join.
features: Instant Algolia search and faceted filters
Scalable catalog with thousands of SKUs

testimonial.quote: ShipFactory understood the marketplace model end to end. We onboarded 40 vendors in our first quarter without a hitch.
testimonial.author: Co-founder, Verde Living
